message:
feat(wizard): add pinned ref toggle to installer command builder (bd-31ps.8.2)

- Add checkbox toggle for "Pin to specific version" option
- Dynamic command generation with ACFS_REF environment variable
- Ref input field with guidance on tags vs SHAs vs main
- Update command breakdown to explain ACFS_REF when pinning

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -930,12 +930,12 @@

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users understand why preflight might fail and how to fix it.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:32:38.536120967Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:32:38.536120967Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.7.4","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:32:38.536120967Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-31ps.8","title":"Pinned ref UX (installer flag + wizard toggle)","description":"## Background
Reproducible installs are safer, but beginners rarely pin refs. A simple UX can make pinning the default when desired.

## Goal
Add a pinning UX: installer flag + wizard toggle to use tagged releases or commit SHAs.

## Scope
- install.sh: add --pin-ref or --confirm-ref flag that prints resolved SHA and optionally persists it.
- Wizard: toggle for pinned install and copy command with ACFS_REF set.

## Testing
- Unit test for ref resolution output.
- Web E2E for toggle + command generation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users can easily pin installs without manual edits.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:32:57.446034043Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:32:57.446034043Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.8","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:32:57.446034043Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-31ps.8.1","title":"Installer: pin-ref flag + resolved SHA output","description":"## Goal
Add installer flag for pinning refs and printing resolved SHA.

## Tasks
- Add `--pin-ref` or `--confirm-ref` flag in install.sh.
- On flag, resolve ACFS_REF to SHA and print a copy-pasteable pinned command.
- Ensure behavior is no-op for non-curl runs unless requested.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users can pin to tags/SHAs with a single flag.
- Output includes resolved SHA and example command.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:33:16.538648751Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T23:40:09.659300005Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T23:40:09.659270098Z","close_reason":"Implemented --pin-ref flag with SHA resolution and copy-paste command output","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:33:16.538648751Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-31ps.9","title":"Auto-resume hint on module failure","description":"## Background
When a module fails, users are unsure how to resume. The selection system can provide a precise resume command.

## Goal
Print an auto-resume hint on failure with exact flags (ACFS_REF, --only/--skip) and context.

## Scope
- install.sh failure handling: compute and print resume command.
- Include the failed phase/module, last successful phase, and a copyable command.

## Testing
- Unit test for resume command formatting.
- VM test: simulated failure emits correct hint.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users see a clear, copyable resume command on failure.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:35:10.660462385Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:35:10.660462385Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.9","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:35:10.660462385Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-31ps.9.1","title":"Installer: compute + print resume hint","description":"## Goal
Implement auto-resume hint generation in install.sh failure path.

## Tasks
- When run_phase fails, compute the most precise resume command: include --only for failed module or --resume with ACFS_REF if set.
- Include mode, target ubuntu version, and skip flags if they were used.
- Print in a copyable block with brief explanation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Failure output always includes a deterministic next command.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:35:27.697001125Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T23:40:16.358854761Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T23:40:16.358835624Z","close_reason":"Implemented generate_resume_hint() and print_resume_hint() functions in install.sh.  {"id":"bd-31sj","title":"Deep exploration: CASS (Coding Agent Session Search)","description":"## Goal
Perform deep exploration of CASS (Coding Agent Session Search) and revise its description on the flywheel/TLDR pages with comprehensive testing.

## Phase 0: Pre-flight Verification (CRITICAL)

### 0.1 Tool Existence Check
```bash
# Verify CASS installation
[[ -d /dp/coding_agent_session_search ]] && echo "PASS: cass repo exists" || { echo "FAIL: cass repo missing"; exit 1; }
command -v cass &>/dev/null && echo "PASS: cass command available" || { echo "FAIL: cass not in PATH"; exit 1; }

# Verify session data exists
[[ -d ~/.claude/projects ]] && echo "PASS: Claude projects directory exists" || echo "WARN: No session data yet"
```

### 0.2 Content Snapshot (BEFORE State)
```bash
SNAPSHOT_DIR=/tmp/cass-exploration-snapshots-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
mkdir -p $SNAPSHOT_DIR
cp apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before
cp apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before
echo "Snapshots saved to $SNAPSHOT_DIR"
```

### 0.3 TypeScript Interface Reference
Content must match FlywheelTool interface:
- id, name, tagline, description, deepDescription
- features[], cliCommands[], connectsTo[]
- integrationLevel, category, status

## Phase 1: Research (GATHER CONTEXT)

### 1.1 Primary Documentation
- `cat /dp/coding_agent_session_search/README.md` - Read full README
- Check for search syntax docs, session format specs

### 1.2 Code Investigation
- Launch code investigation agent to understand:
  - Session discovery across ~/.claude/projects/
  - Index building (SQLite FTS5) and search mechanisms
  - Output formats (--robot mode JSON)
  - Session metadata extraction (timestamps, tools used, context)
  - How it handles large session histories

### 1.3 CLI Command Verification
```bash
# Verify each documented command works
cass --help 2>&1 | head -20
cass search --help 2>&1 | head -20
cass index --help 2>&1 | head -10

# Test actual functionality
cass search "test" --limit 1 --robot 2>&1 | head -5
```

### 1.4 External Context Search
- `/xf search 'cass OR session search OR coding agent'` - Twitter archive
- `cass search 'cass session mining' --robot --limit 10` - Past sessions (meta\!)

### 1.5 Project State Review
- Check beads in /dp/coding_agent_session_search/.beads/
- Review recent commits: `cd /dp/coding_agent_session_search && git log --oneline -20`

## Phase 2: Analysis (SYNTHESIZE UNDERSTANDING)

### 2.1 Core Capabilities Verification
Document findings for each area:
- [ ] Session discovery mechanism (glob patterns, directory traversal)
- [ ] Index structure and storage (SQLite FTS5 schema)
- [ ] Search query syntax (FTS5 operators, phrase matching)
- [ ] Robot mode output schema (JSON fields)
- [ ] Performance on large histories (indexing time, query speed)

### 2.2 Synergy Verification
Cross-reference these tools actually integrate:
- [ ] cm (CASS Memory) - imports cass for session mining
- [ ] xf - similar search patterns
- [ ] mail - message search comparison
- [ ] ntm - session management

### 2.3 Claim Verification
```bash
# Verify session discovery claims
find ~/.claude/projects -name "*.jsonl" 2>/dev/null | wc -l
echo "Total session files found"

# Verify FTS5 capability
sqlite3 ~/.local/share/cass/sessions.db ".schema" 2>/dev/null | grep -i fts
```

## Phase 3: Revision (UPDATE DESCRIPTIONS)

### 3.1 Update apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
Update cass entry with VERIFIED information:
- `tagline`: Accurate one-liner from README
- `description`: Session discovery, indexing, search
- `deepDescription`: How it traverses ~/.claude/projects/
- `features`: Verified search capabilities
- `cliCommands`: Only commands that actually work
- `connectsTo`: Only verified integrations

### 3.2 Update apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
Update TldrFlywheelTool entry with:
- `briefDescription`: Technical summary
- `bulletPoints`: Verified capabilities
- `synergyExamples`: Working integration examples

## Phase 4: Testing (VERIFY CHANGES)

### 4.1 TypeScript Compilation
```bash
cd apps/web && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20
```

### 4.2 Unit Tests
```bash
# Test cass entry structure
node -e "
const { flywheelTools } = require('./lib/flywheel');
const cass = flywheelTools.find(t => t.id === 'cass');
console.log('Testing cass entry...');
console.assert(cass, 'cass entry exists');
console.assert(cass.features?.length > 0, 'has features');
console.assert(cass.cliCommands?.length > 0, 'has commands');
console.assert(cass.connectsTo?.length > 0, 'has connections');
console.log('All assertions passed');
"
```

### 4.3 E2E Test: Full Search Pipeline
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
LOG=/tmp/cass-e2e-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log

echo "=== CASS E2E Test ===" | tee $LOG
echo "Started: $(date)" | tee -a $LOG

# Test 1: Index build/refresh
echo "Test 1: Index operation..." | tee -a $LOG
cass index --force 2>&1 | tee -a $LOG

# Test 2: Basic search
echo "Test 2: Basic search..." | tee -a $LOG
cass search "function" --limit 5 2>&1 | tee -a $LOG

# Test 3: Robot mode output
echo "Test 3: Robot mode JSON..." | tee -a $LOG
cass search "test" --robot --limit 3 2>&1 | python3 -m json.tool | tee -a $LOG

# Test 4: Phrase search
echo "Test 4: Phrase search..." | tee -a $LOG
cass search '"error handling"' --limit 3 2>&1 | tee -a $LOG

echo "=== All Tests Passed ===" | tee -a $LOG
echo "Log: $LOG"
```

### 4.4 Content Diff Verification
```bash
echo "=== Changes Made ==="
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts || true
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts || true
```

## Phase 5: Completion (FINALIZE)

### 5.1 Rollback Procedure (if tests fail)
```bash
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
echo "Rolled back to pre-exploration state"
```

### 5.2 Sync Changes
```bash
br update bd-31sj --status done
br sync --flush-only
```

### 5.3 Final Verification
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors
- [ ] Content matches verified tool capabilities
- [ ] Synergies are reciprocal (cass->cm and cm->cass)
- [ ] No broken links or references","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:00:50.325460661Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T21:31:11.109814287Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-331g","title":"Dark mode toggle in web wizard","description":"## Overview
Add dark mode toggle to the web wizard with system preference detection and localStorage persistence.

## Current Problem
- Web wizard is light-mode only
- Users coding at night prefer dark mode
- No respect for system preference
- Common feature expectation in 2026

## Proposed Behavior
1. Default: Follow system preference (prefers-color-scheme)
2. Toggle: Manual override in header
3. Persist: Save preference to localStorage
4. Instant: No flash of wrong theme on load

## Implementation Details
1. Use CSS custom properties for colors
2. Add `dark` class to `<html>` element
3. Toggle button in header (sun/moon icon)
4. Script in `<head>` to prevent flash

## Color Tokens
```css
:root {
  --bg-primary: #ffffff;
  --text-primary: #1a1a1a;
  --accent: #3b82f6;
}

:root.dark {
  --bg-primary: #0a0a0a;
  --text-primary: #fafafa;
  --accent: #60a5fa;
}
```

## Flash Prevention
```html
<script>
  // In <head>, before CSS loads
  const theme = localStorage.getItem('theme') ||
    (matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches ? 'dark' : 'light');
  document.documentElement.classList.toggle('dark', theme === 'dark');
</script>
```

## Toggle Component
```tsx
function ThemeToggle() {
  const [theme, setTheme] = useTheme();
  return (
    <button onClick={() => setTheme(theme === 'dark' ? 'light' : 'dark')}>
      {theme === 'dark' ? <SunIcon /> : <MoonIcon />}
    </button>
  );
}
```

## Test Plan
- [ ] Test system preference detection
- [ ] Test toggle persists across refreshes
- [ ] Test no flash on page load
- [ ] Test all components render correctly in both modes
- [ ] Visual regression tests

## Files to Modify
- apps/web/app/layout.tsx (theme script)
- apps/web/app/globals.css (color tokens)
- apps/web/components/ui/theme-toggle.tsx (new)
- All components using hardcoded colors","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:02:33.391611994Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T22:58:20.149854699Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T22:58:16.784064183Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-331g","depends_on_id":"bd-3y1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:04:43.692504127Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":41,"issue_id":"bd-331g","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented dark/light/system theme toggle. Hook: useTheme.ts, component: theme-toggle.tsx, flash-prevention in layout.tsx. Commit 5f19854.","created_at":"2026-01-26T22:58:20Z"}]}
